product_results.title: FitVille Wide Walking Shoes for Men Extra Wide Sneakers with Arch Support Cushioning Running Shoes for Flat Feet Wide Toe Box Shoes for Heel Pain Relief Plantar Fasciitis

summary.text: Customers find these shoes comfortable, particularly noting they work well for wider feet. The fit receives positive feedback, with users appreciating the nice wide fit and noting that the size runs true. The quality is consistently praised, with customers describing them as good shoes with bonne qualité. Durability stands out as a strong point, as the shoes are very durable and hold their shape well. Support is another area where customers are satisfied, with the shoes providing great support and soutient le pied effectively. Customers also express happiness with the appearance and weight of the shoes.

authors_reviews.author: steeve

authors_reviews.title: Comfortable with caution.
authors_reviews.text: I have bought zero drop shows from Altra for around 10 years and truly enjoyed them. Unfortunately they are so expensive I also wore them too long and after years of this activity plus my age developed plantar fasciitis, heel spurs, and peroneal tendinitis. At its worst the plantar fasciitis made it hard to walk on my heel and made walking almost impossible until is settles down, and it flairs up. The peroneal tendinitis is a constant but semi manageable pain.So LOTS to ask out of a shoe that claims it can help with at least plantar fasciitis and make walking more comfortable. Especially given that is is less than 50% the cost of any name brand shoe manufacturer.I believe I have the correct size for my foot based off the websites measurement system. I bought size 10 which typical for my other brand and went with just wide which was the narrowest available.The width is actually slightly wide for me but manageable and allows my toes the room they need to spread out like the Altra shoes. The shoe ride up on my ankle a bit though which was /issue causing pain. To help alleviate this i have undine the top lace which was under laced and switched to a traditial x lace and this has helped relive most of this pain.The first thing I noticed was a lot more support (non injured foot) and moderate peroneal tendinitis pain (injured foot). The arch on the injured foot was OKish but definitely felt the pressure of the arch. I semi expected this though since that foot is the nine suffering. But each day (1 day used for a couple hours in house, 2 days full time walking) the pain has lessened as that foot gets used to the added arch and side support.My good foot in the other hand just enjoys the ride, zero issues with comfort. So for those hurting, these may not be an immediate cure and may need time to break in and get your foot get used to the shoe.Outside of the comfort/pain aspect of the shoes. I find these more structured than what I tend to wear but not excessively so. I also find them to be very stable under foot. I live in Alberta Canada which is currently in a deep freeze with lots of loose and packed snow with ice and these have been almost as stable as my latest pair of boots. That is to say more stable than Altra shows or other brands of runners I have tried but not quite as good as boots designed for winter conditions.I will update this review as I get more used to the shoe and will hopefully be able say they have helped some of my conditions over the next month or so.So am giving these a cautiously optimistic 4 for now and will adjust as I go along.
authors_reviews.author: Dweaver
